question: In an Agentforce agent, which building block determines how to respond, which subagent or action to use, and how to complete a task?

options.A: Connections and channels
options.B: The reasoning engine
options.C: Subagents and actions

rationale: The reasoning engine helps the agent determine how to respond and which subagent or action to use.

question: Which Agentforce building block determines how the agent connects to users, employees, customers, and systems?

options.A: Connections and channels
options.B: The reasoning engine
options.C: Data

rationale: Connections and channels determine how the agent connects to users, employees, customers, and systems.

question: Why does selecting the right Agentforce agent type matter when building an agent?

options.A: It sets the LLM sampling temperature
options.B: It chooses the vector embedding model
options.C: It can affect the templates, permissions, channels, setup requirements, and limitations that apply
options.D: It selects the keyword ranking algorithm

rationale: The selected agent type can affect templates, permissions, channels, setup requirements, and limitations.

question: How do Agentforce Data Libraries improve the accuracy of AI features like agents?

options.A: By fine-tuning the underlying LLM on customer data
options.B: By connecting them to trusted data sources so responses are grounded in approved business information
options.C: By increasing the model's maximum output tokens
options.D: By disabling the Einstein Trust Layer for faster responses

rationale: Data libraries connect agents to trusted sources so responses use approved business information, not just the model's general knowledge.

question: Which retriever type defines a placeholder variable for a value that is specified in a prompt template at run time?

options.A: Ensemble Retriever
options.B: Dynamic Retriever
options.C: Search Index

rationale: A dynamic retriever defines a placeholder variable for a value specified in a prompt template at run time.

question: Which retriever executes multiple individual retrievers, combines their results, and reranks them by relevance?

options.A: Dynamic Retriever
options.B: Ensemble Retriever
options.C: Hybrid Autodrop

rationale: An ensemble retriever runs individual retrievers, combines and reranks their results, and returns the most relevant information.

question: What is the role of the hybrid search fusion ranker in Data 360?

options.A: It masks PII before prompts are sent to the LLM
options.B: It reranks the merged keyword and vector results to surface the most relevant
options.C: It generates vector embeddings from unstructured data
options.D: It filters out results that show a sharp drop in relevance

rationale: The fusion ranker reranks the merged keyword and vector results to surface the most relevant results.

question: What does hybrid search autodrop do?

options.A: Dynamically filters out results that show a sharp decrease in relevance scores
options.B: Merges keyword and vector result sets
options.C: Defines a placeholder variable at run time

rationale: Autodrop dynamically filters out results with a sharp decrease in relevance, increasing precision.

question: Which prompt template type lets you define your own resources?

options.A: Sales Email
options.B: Field Generation
options.C: Flex

rationale: A Flex prompt template lets you define your own resources.

question: Which prompt template type brings generative AI-assisted workflows to fields within Lightning record pages?

options.A: Field Generation
options.B: Flex
options.C: Record Summary

rationale: A Field Generation prompt template brings generative AI-assisted workflows to fields within Lightning record pages.

question: What does Einstein Trust Layer data masking do, and when?

options.A: Identifies and masks sensitive information such as PII in prompts before sending them to the LLM
options.B: Encrypts the LLM's response after it is generated
options.C: Fine-tunes the LLM so it forgets PII
options.D: Deletes audit logs to protect privacy

rationale: The Trust Layer identifies and masks sensitive information such as PII in prompts before sending them to the LLM.

question: Where are Einstein Trust Layer toxicity scores logged and stored?

options.A: In the prompt template
options.B: In Data 360 as part of the audit trail
options.C: Only in the external LLM provider's logs

rationale: Toxicity scores are logged and stored in Data 360 as part of the audit trail.

question: After an agent has been activated, how do you make further changes to it?

options.A: Edit the active agent directly in production
options.B: Create a new agent version, then modify and test before activating
options.C: Delete the agent and rebuild it from scratch
options.D: Switch the active agent to draft without versioning

rationale: When updates are needed after activation, create a new agent version so changes can be made and tested before activation.

question: When a prompt template is deployed to another org via a change set, does the version auto-activate?

options.A: Yes, it always auto-activates
options.B: No, unless the Deploy Prompt Templates permission is enabled in the target org
options.C: Yes, but only for Flex templates
options.D: No, and it can never be activated after deployment

rationale: A version does not auto-activate during deployment unless the Deploy Prompt Templates permission is enabled in the target org.

question: What does the SOMA architecture pattern stand for?

options.A: Single Org, Multiple Agents
options.B: Shared Orchestration for Multi-Agents
options.C: Service-Oriented Multi-Agent

rationale: SOMA means Single Org, Multiple Agents, where multiple agents collaborate within one org with shared governance and data.

question: What is the Agent2Agent (A2A) protocol?

options.A: A Salesforce-only message bus between subagents within a single org
options.B: An open standard that lets AI agents from different platforms securely communicate and collaborate
options.C: A data masking technique in the Einstein Trust Layer
options.D: A prompt template type for drafting email

rationale: A2A is an open standard that allows AI agents from different platforms to securely communicate and collaborate.

question: What is Agent Script in Agentforce Builder?

options.A: A natural-language-only chat interface for end users
options.B: The language for building agents, combining natural-language instructions with programmatic expressions
options.C: A reporting tool for agent analytics
options.D: A data masking layer in the Einstein Trust Layer

rationale: Agent Script is the language for building agents in Agentforce Builder; it combines natural-language instructions with programmatic expressions.

question: What capability does Agent Script add that pure natural-language prompts do not provide?

options.A: Controlling when actions run in sequence (action chaining) and executing deterministically
options.B: Increasing the LLM's context window
options.C: Automatically translating the agent into other languages

rationale: Agent Script adds determinism, such as controlling action sequence (action chaining) and when the agent executes deterministically vs. lets the LLM reason.

question: How do Canvas view and Script view relate in the new Agentforce Builder?

options.A: They are separate agents that must be synced manually
options.B: Edits are consistent across both views; the builder writes Agent Script in the background so they stay in sync
options.C: Script view is read-only
options.D: Canvas view is only for viewing analytics

rationale: Configuration and edits are consistent across Canvas and Script views; the builder writes Agent Script in the background so the views stay in sync.

question: What does Commit Version do in the new Agentforce Builder?

options.A: Deploys the agent to all channels
options.B: Saves a snapshot of the agent's metadata and locks the version so you can activate it or create a new draft
options.C: Permanently deletes the current draft
options.D: Runs the agent's Testing Center cases

rationale: Commit Version saves a snapshot of the agent's metadata and locks the version so you can activate the agent or create a new draft.

question: What is the purpose of filters on subagents and actions?

options.A: They make a subagent or action usable only when certain conditions (such as variable values) are met
options.B: They rank search results for grounding
options.C: They mask PII before prompts reach the LLM
options.D: They translate the agent's responses into other languages

rationale: Filters ensure an agent can use a subagent or action only when certain conditions are met, and can limit access based on variables.

question: What is the Agent Router in an Agentforce agent?

options.A: A load balancer that distributes traffic across Salesforce orgs
options.B: A special subagent, set as the starting subagent for every conversation, used for subagent classification
options.C: A retriever type in Data 360
options.D: A channel used to deploy the agent

rationale: The Agent Router is a special subagent added to every agent, defined as the starting subagent and used for subagent classification.

question: When you create a custom action from within an agent in Agentforce Builder (not the asset library), what is its scope?

options.A: It is available to all agents in the org
options.B: It is available only to that agent, and changes apply only to that agent version
options.C: It is automatically added to a managed package

rationale: A custom action created from an agent is available only to that agent, and changes apply only to that agent version; use the asset library to share across agents.

question: For a custom agent action built using a flow, what governs the data and functionality it can access?

options.A: The permissions, field-level security, and sharing settings configured in the flow
options.B: The LLM's training data
options.C: The prompt template's confidence score

rationale: A custom action built on a flow follows the permissions, field-level security, and sharing settings configured in that flow.

question: After an agent is configured, tested, and activated, what makes it available to customers or employees?

options.A: Deploying it to one or more channels, including the Agentforce panel
options.B: Committing a new version
options.C: Adding a filter to a subagent

rationale: An activated agent becomes available to users when it is deployed to one or more channels, including the Agentforce panel.
